subject: docs(user-actions): sync to backend's single-gate revision (tool-till-tees@1f82153)
body:
Backend agent shipped a revision (1f82153) that dropped the
SANCIO_AUTH_BYPASS env var and consolidated to a single-gate bypass:
call-site opt-in (`allowDevBypass: true`) alone, no env var. The sun-
sea-o USER_ACTIONS.md was still describing the env var as "the second
gate" from the two-gate brief — needed to match the shipped reality.

Changes:

- Renamed top entry from "Activate the sancio backend bypass in Vercel"
  to "Sancio backend bypass — active on next deploy (no setup
  required)". The Vercel dashboard step is gone; there's nothing for
  the owner to click.

- Recorded the gate-count choice transparently (not argumentatively):
  original brief specified two gates for safety-net + dashboard-
  flippable kill switch; backend chose single-gate; trade-off is
  consistent with this project's "one environment" policy (bypass is
  always on anyway) but means the removal path is code-change-only
  rather than env-var-flippable. Future-me or future-owner can read
  this and know why it's like this.

- Updated the removal-path steps in BOTH entries (top and the
  "Re-enable authentication" one below): backend removal is now
  "delete the allowDevBypass block from lib/auth.ts + strip the option
  from 25 sancio call sites" rather than "remove env var, optional
  code-delete later."

- Kept the coordinated-flip requirement intact — frontend and backend
  must flip together, same release window, or one side exposes the
  other.

No code change. Docs only.

Tags: docs, auth, coordination, user-actions
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: auth-bypass-coordination
Semver: patch
